title: Organisms & Life Processes
objective: Students will explore organisms & life processes concepts including Animal Life Stages, Seeds & Plant Growth, What Plants Need to Grow and more.

steps.content: Have you ever thought about Animal Life Stages? Let's find out together!
Recognise that animals, including humans, have offspring which grow into adults, and describe basic animal life stages
Time to try this out: Describe the basic life stages of a human: baby, child, teenager, adult
Can you describe how a kitten grows into a cat, or how a baby grows into an adult?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Animal Life Stages. Describe the basic life stages of a human: baby, child, teenager, adult Give examples of other animals and their young (e.g. kitten → cat, chick → hen)
Let's explore Seeds & Plant Growth! Have you ever wondered about this?
Observe and describe how seeds and bulbs grow into mature plants through stages of germination and growth
Let me show you something cool. Describe the stages: seed absorbs water → root appears → shoot pushes up → leaves open
Has you planted a seed or bulb and been able to describe what happened as it grew — first a root, then a shoot, then leaves?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Seeds & Plant Growth. Describe the stages: seed absorbs water → root appears → shoot pushes up → leaves open Compare how a bulb and a seed start growing differently
Have you ever thought about What Plants Need to Grow? Let's find out together!
Understand that plants need water, light, and a suitable temperature to grow and stay healthy
Time to try this out: Name three things plants need to grow: water, light, suitable temperature
Can you explain why a plant on the windowsill grows better than one left in a dark cupboard?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about What Plants Need to Grow. Name three things plants need to grow: water, light, suitable temperature Predict what happens to a plant kept in the dark, with no water, or in freezing cold
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Offspring resemble parents!
Observe that young plants and animals resemble their parents but are not identical, recognising inherited similarities and individual differences
Let's practice together! Describe at least three features that offspring inherit from parents (e.g. eye colour, petal colour, fur type)
Can you look at a family photo and point out features children share with their parents, while also noticing how each child looks a bit different?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Offspring resemble parents: Describe at least three features that offspring inherit from parents (e.g. eye colour, petal colour, fur type) Explain that offspring are similar to parents but not identical copies
